tasks.title: Fix Critical Deployment Blockers
tasks.description: Address three critical deployment blockers: repair the health check API endpoint returning incorrect status codes, fix the broken test suite, and create a .env.example file for deployment configuration.
tasks.details:
This task involves addressing three critical issues blocking deployment:

1. Health Check API Endpoint:
   - Identify why the health check endpoint is returning 500 status codes instead of appropriate 200 (healthy) or 503 (unhealthy) codes
   - Debug the API route handler for the health check endpoint
   - Check for unhandled exceptions or incorrect status code assignments
   - Ensure proper dependency checking in the health check logic
   - Update the endpoint to correctly reflect system health status

2. Broken Test Suite:
   - Analyze the 18/19 failing tests to identify patterns or common issues
   - Fix test environment configuration if needed
   - Update outdated test assertions or mocks
   - Repair broken test fixtures or dependencies
   - Ensure all tests pass consistently in the CI environment

3. Environment Configuration:
   - Create a .env.example file that documents all required environment variables
   - Include comments explaining each variable's purpose and format
   - Provide sample values where appropriate (avoiding sensitive data)
   - Ensure the example matches the current application requirements
   - Document any environment-specific configurations needed for different deployment targets

The implementation should prioritize maintaining backward compatibility while fixing these issues.
tasks.testStrategy:
To verify this task has been completed successfully:

1. Health Check API Endpoint:
   - Manually test the health check endpoint in development environment
   - Verify it returns 200 when all systems are operational
   - Simulate dependency failures to confirm it returns 503 when appropriate
   - Check logs to ensure no unhandled exceptions
   - Write or update automated tests for the health check endpoint

2. Test Suite:
   - Run the complete test suite locally to verify all 19/19 tests pass
   - Execute tests in the CI environment to confirm they pass consistently
   - Review test coverage to ensure critical paths are tested
   - Document any test modifications for the team

3. Environment Configuration:
   - Validate the .env.example file includes all required variables
   - Attempt a fresh deployment using only the .env.example as a guide
   - Have another team member review the .env.example for completeness
   - Verify no sensitive information is included in the example file

Final verification should include a complete deployment to a staging environment to confirm all blockers have been resolved.

tasks.title: Connect Dashboard to Real Data
tasks.description: Replace all mock/static data in dashboard components (SectionCards, ChartAreaInteractive, DataTable) with real data from database using existing API endpoints and backend functions.
tasks.details:
This task involves transitioning from static mock data to real database data across all dashboard components:

1. SectionCards Component:
   - Identify all instances where mock data is currently used
   - Create or utilize existing API service functions to fetch real data
   - Implement proper data fetching with loading states and error handling
   - Update component props and interfaces to accommodate real data structure
   - Ensure data transformation logic is in place if API response format differs from component requirements

2. ChartAreaInteractive Component:
   - Replace hardcoded chart data with dynamic data from API endpoints
   - Implement data refresh mechanisms (polling or event-based updates if needed)
   - Add appropriate loading indicators during data fetching
   - Handle edge cases like empty datasets or malformed responses
   - Ensure chart scales and axes adjust appropriately to real data ranges

3. DataTable Component:
   - Connect table data source to appropriate API endpoints
   - Implement pagination, sorting, and filtering against real data
   - Update column definitions to match actual data structure
   - Add loading states during data fetching operations
   - Implement error handling for failed API requests

4. General Implementation Notes:
   - Use existing API client/service layer for all data fetching
   - Implement proper state management for loading, error, and data states
   - Add appropriate error boundaries to prevent entire dashboard failure if one component fails
   - Consider implementing data caching strategies to improve performance
   - Ensure all components handle authentication/authorization requirements for data access
tasks.testStrategy:
To verify this task has been completed successfully:

1. Functional Testing:
   - Verify each dashboard component displays actual data from the database
   - Compare displayed data with direct database queries to confirm accuracy
   - Test all interactive features (sorting, filtering, pagination) with real data
   - Verify charts render correctly with various data scenarios (empty, partial, complete datasets)

2. Performance Testing:
   - Measure and document load times before and after implementation
   - Verify dashboard performance with large datasets
   - Test responsiveness during data loading and refreshing

3. Error Handling:
   - Simulate API failures and verify appropriate error states are displayed
   - Test with invalid/malformed data to ensure robust error handling
   - Verify authentication failures are handled gracefully

4. Integration Testing:
   - Verify all components update correctly when database data changes
   - Test dashboard behavior during API latency or slow responses
   - Ensure no console errors occur during normal operation

5. Cross-browser Testing:
   - Verify data loading and display works correctly across supported browsers

tasks.title: Implement Functional CSV Upload UI
tasks.description: Connect existing upload components in the administration page to working API endpoints, add progress indicators, error handling, and success feedback for CSV file processing.
tasks.details:
This task involves enhancing the CSV upload functionality in the administration interface:

1. API Integration:
   - Connect the existing upload component to the appropriate API endpoints
   - Implement proper file validation before upload (file type, size limits, format checking)
   - Create service functions to handle the upload process and communication with backend
   - Ensure proper authentication headers are included in API requests

2. User Feedback Implementation:
   - Add a progress indicator showing upload status (percentage or spinner)
   - Implement error handling with user-friendly error messages for different failure scenarios:
     - File format errors
     - Size limit exceeded
     - Server-side processing errors
     - Network connectivity issues
   - Create success feedback mechanism (toast notification, success message, etc.)
   - Add visual cues for upload states (idle, uploading, success, error)

3. Error Recovery:
   - Implement retry functionality for failed uploads
   - Provide clear guidance to users on how to resolve common upload issues
   - Add logging for debugging upload failures

4. UI/UX Considerations:
   - Ensure the upload component remains responsive during file processing
   - Disable appropriate UI elements during active uploads
   - Implement proper focus management after upload completion
   - Add keyboard accessibility for the entire upload workflow

5. Testing Considerations:
   - Test with various file sizes and formats
   - Verify all error scenarios are properly handled
   - Ensure the UI remains responsive during uploads
tasks.testStrategy:
To verify this task has been completed successfully:

1. Functional Testing:
   - Verify CSV files can be selected and uploaded through the UI
   - Confirm progress indicators accurately reflect upload status
   - Test with valid CSV files to ensure successful processing
   - Test with invalid files (wrong format, corrupted, too large) to verify error handling
   - Verify appropriate success messages appear after successful uploads
   - Check that uploaded data appears correctly in the system after processing

2. UI/UX Testing:
   - Verify the upload component remains responsive during file processing
   - Confirm that appropriate UI elements are disabled during active uploads
   - Test keyboard navigation through the entire upload workflow
   - Verify focus management after upload completion
   - Check that all states (idle, uploading, success, error) have appropriate visual indicators

3. Error Handling Testing:
   - Simulate network failures during upload to verify error handling
   - Test with malformed CSV files to ensure proper validation and error messages
   - Verify retry functionality works correctly for failed uploads
   - Check that error messages are clear and provide actionable guidance

4. Performance Testing:
   - Test with various file sizes to ensure the UI remains responsive
   - Verify large files are handled appropriately with accurate progress indication

5. Cross-browser Testing:
   - Verify functionality works consistently across supported browsers

tasks.title: Connect Filters to Real Data API Endpoints
tasks.description: Implement working filter functionality by connecting existing filter UI components to backend API endpoints with real-time data filtering and search capabilities.
tasks.details:
This task involves connecting the existing filter UI components to backend API endpoints to enable real-time data filtering and search:

1. API Integration:
   - Identify all filter components across the application that need to be connected to real data
   - Map each filter type (dropdown, range slider, search box, date picker, etc.) to appropriate API parameters
   - Create or extend service functions to handle parameterized API requests with filter criteria
   - Implement debouncing for search inputs to prevent excessive API calls
   - Ensure filter state is properly maintained in the application state management system

2. Filter Request Implementation:
   - Modify existing API service functions to accept filter parameters
   - Implement URL query parameter construction for RESTful filtering
   - Create transformers to convert UI filter state to API-compatible parameters
   - Add support for multiple concurrent filter criteria (combined filtering)
   - Implement proper error handling for filter requests

3. Real-time Updates:
   - Implement loading states during filter operations
   - Add visual feedback when filters are applied or cleared
   - Ensure filtered results update the relevant components (DataTable, charts, etc.)
   - Maintain filter state during navigation or component remounting
   - Implement filter reset functionality

4. Performance Considerations:
   - Implement pagination for filtered results to handle large datasets
   - Consider server-side vs. client-side filtering based on data volume
   - Optimize filter requests to minimize payload size
   - Add caching for frequently used filter combinations if appropriate

5. Filter State Management:
   - Store active filters in application state
   - Implement filter history or recently used filters if required
   - Enable filter state to be shareable via URLs
   - Persist filter preferences in user settings if applicable
tasks.testStrategy:
To verify this task has been completed successfully:

1. Functional Testing:
   - Test each filter type individually to ensure it correctly filters data
   - Verify combined filters work properly (e.g., date range + search term + dropdown selection)
   - Confirm that clearing filters restores the original unfiltered data view
   - Test edge cases like empty result sets, all items matching, etc.
   - Verify search functionality works with partial matches and different text cases

2. API Integration Testing:
   - Use browser network tools to confirm filter parameters are correctly sent to API
   - Verify API requests contain the expected parameters based on UI filter selections
   - Check that error responses from the API are properly handled in the UI
   - Test with mock API delays to ensure loading states display correctly

3. Performance Testing:
   - Test with large datasets to ensure filtering remains responsive
   - Verify that debouncing works correctly for search inputs
   - Check that pagination works correctly with filtered results

4. UI/UX Testing:
   - Confirm appropriate loading indicators display during filter operations
   - Verify filter state is visually represented (showing which filters are active)
   - Test that filter components are accessible and keyboard navigable
   - Ensure mobile responsiveness of filter UI components

5. Regression Testing:
   - Verify that existing dashboard functionality continues to work with filters applied
   - Check that filtered data correctly updates all connected visualizations and tables

subtasks.description: Connect existing filter components to backend API endpoints for real-time data filtering

tasks.title: Build Analytics Report Generation System
tasks.description: Create at least one working report (player performance or traffic analysis) with export functionality, charts, and data visualization using existing Recharts components.
tasks.details:
This task involves implementing a comprehensive analytics report generation system:

1. Report Framework Development:
   - Create a modular report template system that can be extended for different report types
   - Implement a report configuration interface to define data sources, visualization types, and export options
   - Develop a report rendering engine that can generate both on-screen and exportable reports

2. Data Integration:
   - Connect to existing API endpoints to fetch relevant analytics data
   - Implement data transformation functions to prepare raw data for visualization
   - Create data aggregation utilities for summarizing information across different time periods
   - Ensure proper error handling for data fetching and processing

3. Visualization Implementation:
   - Utilize existing Recharts components to create meaningful data visualizations
   - Implement at least two chart types (line, bar, pie, or area) for the initial report
   - Add interactive elements like tooltips, zoom functionality, and clickable data points
   - Ensure visualizations are responsive and maintain readability across device sizes

4. Export Functionality:
   - Implement PDF export capability using a library like jsPDF or react-pdf
   - Add CSV/Excel export option for raw data tables
   - Create export configuration options (paper size, orientation, included sections)
   - Ensure exported reports maintain visual fidelity and proper formatting

5. User Interface:
   - Design an intuitive report configuration panel with options for data filtering
   - Implement a preview mode for reports before export
   - Add loading states and error handling for report generation process
   - Create a reports history/saved reports section if applicable
tasks.testStrategy:
To verify this task has been completed successfully:

1. Functional Testing:
   - Verify that at least one complete report type (player performance or traffic analysis) can be generated
   - Confirm all charts and visualizations correctly display data from the API
   - Test interactive elements like tooltips, filters, and date range selectors
   - Ensure the export functionality produces well-formatted PDF and CSV/Excel files
   - Validate that exported reports contain all the same data as the on-screen version

2. Data Accuracy Testing:
   - Compare the data displayed in reports with direct database queries to confirm accuracy
   - Test with different date ranges and filter combinations to ensure consistent results
   - Verify aggregated metrics are calculated correctly according to business requirements

3. Performance Testing:
   - Measure report generation time with various data set sizes
   - Test with simulated large data sets to ensure the system remains responsive
   - Verify that long-running report generation doesn't block the UI

4. UI/UX Testing:
   - Confirm the report configuration interface is intuitive and user-friendly
   - Test the responsiveness of reports on different screen sizes
   - Verify accessibility standards are met for all report components

5. Integration Testing:
   - Ensure the report system works correctly with the authentication system
   - Verify proper integration with existing dashboard components
   - Test that filter changes propagate correctly to the report data

tasks.title: Set Up Production Deployment Infrastructure
tasks.description: Configure Docker, environment variables, DigitalOcean deployment scripts, database migrations, and production optimizations to prepare the application for stable production deployment.
tasks.details:
This task involves setting up the complete production deployment infrastructure:

1. Docker Configuration:
   - Create a production-ready Dockerfile optimized for the application
   - Set up docker-compose.yml for orchestrating multiple services (application, database, cache)
   - Implement multi-stage builds to minimize final image size
   - Configure proper caching strategies for dependencies
   - Set up volume mappings for persistent data
   - Define appropriate network configurations for service communication

2. Environment Variables Management:
   - Extend the .env.example file from Task 1 with production-specific configurations
   - Implement a secure strategy for managing sensitive credentials in production
   - Set up environment variable validation at application startup
   - Configure different environment profiles (development, staging, production)
   - Document all required environment variables and their purposes

3. DigitalOcean Deployment Scripts:
   - Create deployment scripts for provisioning DigitalOcean resources (Droplets, Databases, etc.)
   - Set up CI/CD pipeline configuration for automated deployments
   - Implement blue-green deployment strategy to minimize downtime
   - Configure proper resource allocation based on expected load
   - Set up monitoring and logging integrations
   - Create rollback procedures for failed deployments

4. Database Migrations:
   - Implement a robust database migration strategy for zero-downtime updates
   - Create scripts for automated database backups before migrations
   - Set up validation checks to ensure data integrity after migrations
   - Configure proper database connection pooling for production
   - Implement database seeding for initial production data if required

5. Production Optimizations:
   - Configure proper caching strategies (Redis, CDN, browser caching)
   - Implement asset compression and minification
   - Set up rate limiting and request throttling
   - Configure proper logging levels for production
   - Implement performance monitoring
   - Set up automated health checks and alerting
   - Configure proper security headers and CORS policies
tasks.testStrategy:
To verify this task has been completed successfully:

1. Docker Configuration Testing:
   - Build the Docker image using the production configuration
   - Verify the image size is optimized and unnecessary development dependencies are excluded
   - Test container startup and proper initialization of all services
   - Validate that all required volumes are correctly mounted
   - Verify proper network communication between containerized services

2. Environment Variables Testing:
   - Validate that the application correctly loads and uses environment variables
   - Test application behavior when required variables are missing or malformed
   - Verify that sensitive information is properly protected
   - Test switching between different environment profiles

3. DigitalOcean Deployment Testing:
   - Execute deployment scripts in a staging environment
   - Verify resource provisioning works as expected
   - Test the CI/CD pipeline with a sample deployment
   - Validate that blue-green deployment correctly switches traffic
   - Test rollback procedures to ensure they work as expected

4. Database Migration Testing:
   - Execute migration scripts against a clone of production data
   - Verify data integrity before and after migrations
   - Measure migration execution time to ensure it meets requirements
   - Test backup and restore procedures
   - Validate connection pooling under load

5. Production Optimization Testing:
   - Perform load testing to verify system performance under expected traffic
   - Validate caching mechanisms are working correctly
   - Test CDN integration and asset delivery
   - Verify security headers and CORS policies
   - Monitor resource usage (CPU, memory, disk I/O) under load
   - Test automated health checks and alerting mechanisms
